statement: This record remains negative evidence on build 26100: the repo did not produce enough supporting proof to promote it into a normal actionable surface.
gating_reason: Archived audit trail only. Keep this out of the normal tweak surface.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: SystemParametersInfoW function
links.summary: Microsoft documents the shutdown timeout concepts SPI_GETHUNGAPPTIMEOUT, SPI_GETWAITTOKILLTIMEOUT, and SPI_GETWAITTOKILLSERVICETIMEOUT with default values of 5000 ms, 5000 ms, and 20000 ms respectively.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: RM_SHUTDOWN_TYPE
links.summary: Microsoft documents that forced shutdown timing for applications and services is tied to the hung app timeout and wait-to-kill service timeout system parameters.

links.title: Microsoft Learn: Service Control Handler Function
links.summary: Microsoft directly documents HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\WaitToKillServiceTimeout as the service shutdown timeout used during operating system restart and advises that services should not raise it on their own.

links.title: Repo system research notes for shutdown timeouts
links.summary: The repo documents the mixed timeout bundle, including HungAppTimeout = 1500, WaitToKillServiceTimeout = 2500, and a note that WaitToKillAppTimeout may no longer be used and WaitToKillTimeout is the more relevant concept.

links.title: Current app implementation
links.summary: The old app implementation wrote WaitToKillServiceTimeout = 2500, WaitToKillAppTimeout = 2500, HungAppTimeout = 1500, and AutoEndTasks = 1 as one bundle.
